# ExFig Project Conventions

These rules apply when working in projects that use ExFig for Figma design export.

## Configuration

- Config file: `exfig.pkl` at project root
- Config format: PKL (Programmable, Scalable, Safe) — NOT YAML, NOT JSON
- Schema: `amends "package://github.com/DesignPipe/exfig/...#/ExFig.pkl"`
- Validate before commit: `exfig validate exfig.pkl`

## Generated Files

- **Never manually edit** files generated by ExFig (colors, icons, images, typography)
- Generated files are identified by comments like `// Generated by ExFig — do not edit`
- If you need to modify generated output, change the config or Jinja templates instead
- Commit generated files to version control (they should be reproducible but tracked)

## Naming Conventions

### iOS
- Color assets: match Figma variable names, kebab-case → camelCase
- Icon assets: match Figma component names, kebab-case preserved in xcassets
- Swift extensions: `Colors.swift`, `Typography.swift` in dedicated theme folder

### Android
- XML color names: match Figma, converted to snake_case
- VectorDrawable files: `ic_{name}.xml` in `res/drawable/`
- Compose files: `Color.kt`, `Type.kt` in theme package

### Flutter
- Dart files: `colors.dart`, `typography.dart` in `lib/theme/`
- SVG icons: `assets/icons/{name}.svg`

### Web
- CSS custom properties: `--color-{name}` prefix
- JSX components: PascalCase icon names

## CI/CD

- Run `exfig batch exfig.pkl --cache` in CI to export and cache
- Use `--force` to ignore cache when needed
- Pin ExFig version in CI to avoid breaking changes
- Store `FIGMA_PERSONAL_TOKEN` as a repository secret, never in code
- Store `PENPOT_ACCESS_TOKEN` as a repository secret when using Penpot source

## Design Source Organization

### Figma
- Use consistent frame names that match config `figmaFrameName` values
- Group related icons in dedicated frames (e.g., "Navigation", "Actions")
- Use Figma Variables for colors (preferred over color styles) — supports themes/modes
- Keep a separate dark mode file or use variable modes for dark colors

### Penpot
- Add all exportable assets to the shared **Library** (not just on canvas)
- Use path groups for organization: `Brand/Primary`, `Icons/Navigation/arrow-left`
- Use `pathFilter` in config to select specific path prefixes
- Typography requires fontSize to be set (styles without it are skipped)
- Icons/images exported as SVG via shape tree reconstruction — supports SVG, PNG (any scale), PDF
- For best SVG quality, use simple shapes (path, rect, circle) — complex effects (blur, shadow) are not yet rendered

## PKL Config Best Practices

- Use `local` variables for DRY config (not duplicated in output)
- Prefer `for`-generators over repeated entries
- Keep one `exfig.pkl` per project (batch mode handles multiple platforms)
- Validate config with `pkl eval --format json exfig.pkl` before commits
